subjectAreas.name: New Testament Writings
subjectAreas.description: Covers the canonical Gospels, Acts, Pauline letters, non-Pauline epistles, and Revelation, including their authorship, sources, dating, historical reliability, textual issues, transmission, and interpretation.

subjectAreas.name: Jesus and Paul
subjectAreas.description: Covers historical study of Jesus and Paul, traditions about Jesus' life and teachings, other New Testament figures, and Paul's life, mission, communities, theology, and ethics.

subjectAreas.name: Early Christianity and Ancient Context
subjectAreas.description: Covers Christian origins, early expansion, non-canonical writings, persecution, martyrdom, church history, the church fathers, and the wider Roman and Greco-Roman setting of early Christianity.

subjectAreas.name: Christian Theology and Belief
subjectAreas.description: Covers Christian understandings of Christ, salvation, orthodoxy and heresy, suffering and evil, faith, doubt, and nonbelief.

subjectAreas.name: Afterlife and Supernatural Claims
subjectAreas.description: Covers beliefs about life after death, resurrection, heaven and hell, miracles, visions, and other supernatural claims.

subjectAreas.name: Ethics and Social Issues
subjectAreas.description: Covers moral reasoning, wealth, poverty, charity, women, gender, sexuality, and related biblical and Christian social questions.

subjectAreas.name: Hebrew Bible and Early Judaism
subjectAreas.description: Covers Hebrew Bible texts and traditions, early Jewish groups, Jewish beliefs, apocalyptic expectations, scripture, law, and the historical development of early Judaism.

subjectAreas.name: Texts, Manuscripts, and Methods
subjectAreas.description: Covers how biblical and early Christian traditions were composed, remembered, attributed, collected, copied, translated, interpreted, and evaluated through historical and textual methods.

subjectAreas.name: Books, Scholarship, and the Blog
subjectAreas.description: Covers Bart's books, teaching, media appearances, academic life, public debates, recurring blog features, and personal posts.
categories: Academic Life, Scholars, and Public Debate
